heading: Charitable remainder trusts

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, the provisions of this section shall, in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary, apply in the case of a charitable remainder annuity trust and a charitable remainder unitrust.

First, as amounts of income (other than gains, and amounts treated as gains, from the sale or other disposition of capital assets) includible in gross income to the extent of such income of the trust for the year and such undistributed income of the trust for prior years;
Second, as a capital gain to the extent of the capital gain of the trust for the year and the undistributed capital gain of the trust for prior years;
Third, as other income to the extent of such income of the trust for the year and such undistributed income of the trust for prior years; and
Fourth, as a distribution of trust corpus.

children.heading: Income tax
children.label: 1 Income tax
children.text: A charitable remainder annuity trust and a charitable remainder unitrust shall, for any taxable year, not be subject to any tax imposed by this subtitle.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of a charitable remainder annuity trust or a charitable remainder unitrust which has unrelated business taxable income (within the meaning of section 512, determined as if part III of subchapter F applied to such trust) for a taxable year, there is hereby imposed on such trust or unitrust an excise tax equal to the amount of such unrelated business taxable income.

children.heading: Certain rules to apply
children.label: B Certain rules to apply
children.text: The tax imposed by subparagraph (A) shall be treated as imposed by chapter 42 for purposes of this title other than subchapter E of chapter 42.

children.heading: Tax court proceedings
children.label: C Tax court proceedings
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the references in section 6212(c)(1) to section 4940 shall be deemed to include references to this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: from which a sum certain (which is not less than 5 percent nor more than 50 percent of the initial net fair market value of all property placed in trust) is to be paid, not less often than annually, to one or more persons (at least one of which is not an organization described in section 170(c) and, in the case of individuals, only to an individual who is living at the time of the creation of the trust) for a term of years (not in excess of 20 years) or for the life or lives of such individual or individuals,

children.label: B
children.text: from which no amount other than the payments described in subparagraph (A) and other than qualified gratuitous transfers described in subparagraph (C) may be paid to or for the use of any person other than an organization described in section 170(c),

children.label: C
children.text: following the termination of the payments described in subparagraph (A), the remainder interest in the trust is to be transferred to, or for the use of, an organization described in section 170(c) or is to be retained by the trust for such a use or, to the extent the remainder interest is in qualified employer securities (as defined in subsection (g)(4)), all or part of such securities are to be transferred to an employee stock ownership plan (as defined in section 4975(e)(7)) in a qualified gratuitous transfer (as defined by subsection (g)), and

children.label: D
children.text: the value (determined under section 7520) of such remainder interest is at least 10 percent of the initial net fair market value of all property placed in the trust.

children.label: A
children.text: from which a fixed percentage (which is not less than 5 percent nor more than 50 percent) of the net fair market value of its assets, valued annually, is to be paid, not less often than annually, to one or more persons (at least one of which is not an organization described in section 170(c) and, in the case of individuals, only to an individual who is living at the time of the creation of the trust) for a term of years (not in excess of 20 years) or for the life or lives of such individual or individuals,

children.label: B
children.text: from which no amount other than the payments described in subparagraph (A) and other than qualified gratuitous transfers described in subparagraph (C) may be paid to or for the use of any person other than an organization described in section 170(c),

children.label: C
children.text: following the termination of the payments described in subparagraph (A), the remainder interest in the trust is to be transferred to, or for the use of, an organization described in section 170(c) or is to be retained by the trust for such a use or, to the extent the remainder interest is in qualified employer securities (as defined in subsection (g)(4)), all or part of such securities are to be transferred to an employee stock ownership plan (as defined in section 4975(e)(7)) in a qualified gratuitous transfer (as defined by subsection (g)), and

children.label: D
children.text: with respect to each contribution of property to the trust, the value (determined under section 7520) of such remainder interest in such property is at least 10 percent of the net fair market value of such property as of the date such property is contributed to the trust.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of the trust income, if such amount is less than the amount required to be distributed under paragraph (2)(A), and

children.label: B
children.text: any amount of the trust income which is in excess of the amount required to be distributed under paragraph (2)(A), to the extent that (by reason of subparagraph (A)) the aggregate of the amounts paid in prior years was less than the aggregate of such required amounts.

children.label: A
children.text: any contribution is made to a trust which before the contribution is a charitable remainder unitrust, and

children.label: B
children.text: such contribution would (but for this paragraph) result in such trust ceasing to be a charitable unitrust by reason of paragraph (2)(D),

children.heading: Valuation of interests
children.label: e Valuation of interests
children.text: For purposes of determining the amount of any charitable contribution, the remainder interest of a charitable remainder annuity trust or charitable remainder unitrust shall be computed on the basis that an amount equal to 5 percent of the net fair market value of its assets (or a greater amount, if required under the terms of the trust instrument) is to be distributed each year. In the case of the early termination of a trust which is a charitable remainder unitrust by reason of subsection (d)(3), the valuation of interests in such trust for purposes of this section shall be made under rules similar to the rules of the preceding sentence.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: If a trust would, but for a qualified contingency, meet the requirements of paragraph (1)(A) or (2)(A) of subsection (d), such trust shall be treated as meeting such requirements.

children.heading: Value determined without regard to qualified contingency
children.label: 2 Value determined without regard to qualified contingency
children.text: For purposes of determining the amount of any charitable contribution (or the actuarial value of any interest), a qualified contingency shall not be taken into account.

children.heading: Qualified contingency
children.label: 3 Qualified contingency
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “qualified contingency” means any provision of a trust which provides that, upon the happening of a contingency, the payments described in paragraph (1)(A) or (2)(A) of subsection (d) (as the case may be) will terminate not later than such payments would otherwise terminate under the trust.

children.heading: Qualified gratuitous transfer of qualified employer securities
children.label: g Qualified gratuitous transfer of qualified employer securities
A
children.text: the securities transferred previously passed from a decedent dying before , to a trust described in paragraph (1) or (2) of subsection (d),January 1, 1999

children.label: B
children.text: no deduction under section 404 is allowable with respect to such transfer,

children.label: C
children.text: such plan contains the provisions required by paragraph (3),

children.label: D
children.text: such plan treats such securities as being attributable to employer contributions but without regard to the limitations otherwise applicable to such contributions under section 404, and

children.label: E
children.text: the employer whose employees are covered by the plan described in this paragraph files with the Secretary a verified written statement consenting to the application of sections 4978 and 4979A with respect to such employer.

children.label: A
children.text: such plan was in existence on ,August 1, 1996

children.label: B
children.text: at the time of the transfer, the decedent and members of the decedent’s family (within the meaning of section 2032A(e)(2)) own (directly or through the application of section 318(a)) no more than 10 percent of the value of the stock of the corporation referred to in paragraph (4), and

children.label: C
children.text: immediately after the transfer, such plan owns (after the application of section 318(a)(4)) at least 60 percent of the value of the outstanding stock of the corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: the qualified employer securities so transferred are allocated to plan participants in a manner consistent with section 401(a)(4),

children.label: B
children.text: plan participants are entitled to direct the plan as to the manner in which such securities which are entitled to vote and are allocated to the account of such participant are to be voted,

children.label: C
children.text: an independent trustee votes the securities so transferred which are not allocated to plan participants,

children.label: D
children.text: each participant who is entitled to a distribution from the plan has the rights described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of section 409(h)(1),

children.label: E
children.text: such securities are held in a suspense account under the plan to be allocated each year, up to the applicable limitation under paragraph (7) (determined on the basis of fair market value of securities when allocated to participants), after first allocating all other annual additions for the limitation year, up to the limitation under section 415(c), and

children.label: F
children.text: on termination of the plan, all securities so transferred which are not allocated to plan participants as of such termination are to be transferred to, or for the use of, an organization described in section 170(c).

children.label: A
children.text: which has no outstanding stock which is readily tradable on an established securities market, and

children.label: B
children.text: which has only 1 class of stock.

children.heading: Treatment of securities allocated by employee stock ownership plan to persons related to decedent or 5-percent shareholders
children.label: 5 Treatment of securities allocated by employee stock ownership plan to persons related to decedent or 5-percent shareholders
i
children.text: any person who is related to the decedent (within the meaning of section 267(b)) or a member of the decedent’s family (within the meaning of section 2032A(e)(2)), or

children.label: ii
children.text: any person who, at the time of such allocation or at any time during the 1-year period ending on the date of the acquisition of qualified employer securities by the plan, is a 5-percent shareholder of the employer maintaining the plan,

children.heading: 5-percent shareholder
children.label: B 5-percent shareholder
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “5-percent shareholder” means any person who owns (directly or through the application of section 318(a)) more than 5 percent of the outstanding stock of the corporation which issued such qualified employer securities or of any corporation which is a member of the same controlled group of corporations (within the meaning of section 409()(4)) as such corporation. For purposes of the preceding sentence, section 318(a) shall be applied without regard to the exception in paragraph (2)(B)(i) thereof.l

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: C Cross reference
children.text: For excise tax on allocations described in subparagraph (A), see section 4979A.

children.heading: Tax on failure to transfer unallocated securities to charity on termination of plan
children.label: 6 Tax on failure to transfer unallocated securities to charity on termination of plan
A
children.text: the amount of the increase in the tax which would be imposed by chapter 11 if such securities were not transferred as described in paragraph (1), and

children.label: B
children.text: interest on such amount at the underpayment rate under section 6621 (and compounded daily) from the due date for filing the return of the tax imposed by chapter 11.

children.label: ii
children.text: 25 percent of the participant’s compensation (as defined in section 415(c)(3)).

children.heading: Cost-of-living adjustment
children.label: B Cost-of-living adjustment
children.text: The Secretary shall adjust annually the $30,000 amount under subparagraph (A)(i) at the same time and in the same manner as under section 415(d), except that the base period shall be the calendar quarter beginning , and any increase under this subparagraph which is not a multiple of $5,000 shall be rounded to the next lowest multiple of $5,000.October 1, 1993
